name: KCL
description: A constraint-based record and functional language for cloud-native configuration and policy scenarios

implementation.comment: 98.8% Rust with 0.9% C code in the implementation

language-family.comment: Draws inspiration from Python and Go, with custom constraint-based syntax

crates-io.comment: Rust SDK distributed via GitHub rather than crates.io

first-class-functions.comment: Lambda functions can be assigned to variables and passed as arguments

closures.comment: Lambda functions support capture of external variables

pattern-matching.comment: No traditional pattern matching; uses quantifier expressions and type guards instead

error-handling.comment: Assertion-based error handling with compile-time validation

async-await.comment: Not supported by design; KCL avoids system-level functions

iterators-generators.comment: Comprehensions and quantifier expressions (all, any, map, filter)

module-system.comment: Package-based structure with OCI registry support for sharing

standard-library.comment: Built-in functions for configuration; no I/O or system functions by design

execution-strategy.comment: Compiled language with native code and WASM compilation support

hot-reloading.comment: Configuration language; not designed for hot reloading scenarios

repl-support.comment: Interactive mode available via kcl CLI

source-maps.comment: Error messages include source location information

execution-speed.comment: Compiled to native code or WASM

memory-overhead.comment: Configuration-focused runtime without heavy interpreter overhead

garbage-collection.comment: Uses garbage collection for memory management

api-safety.comment: Safe Rust API with unsafe contained internally

idiomatic-api.comment: Builder pattern with args structs; functional but somewhat verbose

serde-integration.comment: Returns YAML-formatted results; integrates with Rust data types

function-registration.comment: Uses argument struct builders for API calls

blank-slate-mode.comment: Configuration language with built-in schema and constraint primitives

memory-sandboxing.comment: No access to arbitrary host memory; pure configuration language

capability-security.comment: Not capability-based; simply lacks dangerous operations

default-deny.comment: No I/O or system functions available to scripts

call-rust-from-script.comment: Configuration language; scripts don't call host functions

call-script-from-rust.comment: exec_program executes KCL code and returns results

custom-types.comment: Schemas defined in KCL, not Rust types exposed to scripts

method-syntax.comment: Attribute access on schemas, not custom method definitions

crates-io-downloads.comment: Rust SDK distributed via GitHub, not crates.io

package-manager.comment: Built-in package management with OCI registry support

editor-support.comment: IDE extensions, format, lint, and LSP tooling

example-projects.comment: Used at Ant Group, Huawei for cloud-native configuration

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive website with tutorials, API reference, and language tour

bus-factor.comment: CNCF Sandbox project with 50+ contributors, backed by Ant Group

maturity.comment: Production use at Ant Group, Huawei, Youzan; CNCF Sandbox project

primary-use-cases.comment: Cloud-native configuration, policy definition, Kubernetes orchestration
